claims.subject: node_adapter:record_source_harness
claims.statement: record-source harnesses advance checkpoints only through processed/skipped records and collect material anchors for processed records

claims.subject: scenario:*
claims.statement: proof-carrying scenarios emit subject refs, claim ids, runner id, reproducer, and artifact references

claims.subject: runtime_unit:*
claims.statement: logical source records should flow through bounded physical source-material frames rather than one tiny material per record

claims.subject: runtime_unit:*
claims.statement: runtime units that ingest external records must produce material provenance with stable anchors

claims.subject: source_unit:*
claims.statement: source-unit identity must not imply a new Cargo package, Rust binary target, Nix output, or independent derivation by default

claims.subject: source_unit:*
claims.statement: source-unit manifests expose crate, binary, package-output, derivation, SQLx validation, and service-instance impact

claims.subject: xtask_command:*
claims.statement: public xtask command contracts should be derived from clap introspection and covered by ordinary Rust scenarios

obligations.subject: node_adapter:record_source_harness
obligations.reason: source adapters are the natural SDK boundary for cursor and material-anchor correctness

obligations.subject: runtime_unit:*
obligations.reason: source units are the material-provenance boundary for replay and audit

obligations.subject: scenario:*
obligations.reason: scenario failures must carry enough context to avoid host forensics

obligations.subject: source_unit:*
obligations.reason: source units are the semantic leaves that must carry replayable material provenance

obligations.subject: source_unit:*
obligations.reason: new source units must make build/cache/service impact visible before adding more physical artifacts

obligations.subject: xtask_command:*
obligations.reason: command-surface contracts should live in ordinary test/scenario infrastructure rather than xtask exercise entries
